script.text: Unit 21 introduces Conditionals—the 'If-Then' sentences of Modern Greek. These structures are divided by their level of reality and likelihood. Athina, how do we typically express 'If' in spoken Greek?
Αν, εάν, ή άμα. Το 'άμα' είναι το πιο καθημερινό.
Correct. We categorize conditionals into three types. Type 1 represents real-world possibilities. Athina, please provide an example of a Type 1 conditional.
Αν βρέχει, δεν θα πάμε. Άμα τελειώσεις νωρίς, πάρε με τηλέφωνο.
Note that 'An' clauses are negated with 'Den'. Type 2 conditionals represent dreams or hypotheticals. These are formed by utilizing the Imperfect past tense on both sides of the comma. Athina?
Αν είχα λεφτά, θα αγόραζα ένα νησί. Θα αγόραζα σημαίνει 'I would buy'.

Finally, Type 3 conditionals express regret over past events that cannot be changed. These utilize the Past Perfect tense. However, in casual conversation, Greeks often utilize the Type 2 structure for past regrets. Athina, provide a formal Type 3 example.
Αν είχα διαβάσει, θα είχα περάσει. Αλλά δεν διάβασα.
This concludes our academic review of Unit 21. Developing a mastery of conditional balancing is a critical step towards advanced syntactic proficiency in Modern Greek.
